perturbation.original_text: Owner shall at all times provide sufficient and proper equipment for Charterer's performance of such transportation. Said equipment shall be manned, equipped, supplied and operated by Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: Owner shall endeavor to provide sufficient and proper equipment for Charterer's performance of such transportation. Said equipment may be manned, equipped, supplied and operated by a third-party contractor at Owner's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding the Owner's obligation to provide equipment and who is responsible for operating it. The original text mandates that Owner provides and operates the equipment, while the modified version allows for third-party contractors and changes shall into endeavor which weakens the obligation. This introduces ambiguity by making the agreement unclear whether the owner is soley responsable to provide or if the owner is allowed to use third-party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, Charterer agrees that Owner will be the sole and exclusive provider of marine transportation services for #2 fuel oil and high sulfur diesel owned by Charterer or owned by others and in transit for sale to Charterer so long as Owner has the required equipment available. Owner shall endeavor to provide sufficient and proper equipment for Charterer's performance of such transportation. Said equipment may be manned, equipped, supplied and operated by a third-party contractor at Owner's discretion. The master and crew of said vessels shall be fully qualified, experience and, where necessary, certified and licensed. Owner agrees that said equipment shall be maintained in a seaworthy, staunch, tight and suitable condition and, to the best of Owner's knowledge, in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations. In connection with its use of any vessel, Charterer will follow Owner's normal scheduling, loading and offloading protocols established from time to time, subject to Owner's obligations set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens the Owner's obligation to provide and operate the equipment, directly contradicting the original text that mandates Owner's sole responsibility. The shift to 'endeavor' and allowing third-party contractors creates uncertainty about who is ultimately responsible, conflicting with the clause stating that 'Owner will be the sole and exclusive provider of marine transportation services.' This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the terms are now defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: Charterer agrees to pay to Owner a fee of $______ per gallon of product transported. Owner will invoice such fees to Charterer on a monthly basis and Charterer will pay such invoiced amounts within 30 days of invoice date.
perturbation.changed_text: Charterer agrees to pay to Owner a fee, to be determined by market conditions, per gallon of product transported. Owner will invoice such fees to Charterer on a quarterly basis, and Charterer will pay such invoiced amounts within 60 days of invoice date, pending a satisfactory audit.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity around payment terms. Originally the payment was defined and 30 days of invoice date, with this perturbation the price is determined by market conditions, which could be easily exploited. Along with this change, the new invoice payments are quarterly instead of monthly and 60 days instead of 30. This introduces ambiguity and creates an in-text contradiction regarding payment, the conditions of payment and payment time frame.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RATE Charterer agrees to pay to Owner a fee, to be determined by market conditions, per gallon of product transported. Owner will invoice such fees to Charterer on a quarterly basis, and Charterer will pay such invoiced amounts within 60 days of invoice date, pending a satisfactory audit. The fee stated above, unless otherwise adjusted by the parties pursuant to Section 1 above, shall be adjusted annually (both upward and downward), by a factor equal to the amount of increase or decrease, as the case may be, in the Consumer Price Index for the immediately proceeding month of November, over the Consumer Price Index for November of the preceding year. For purposes hereof, the term "Consumer Price Index" shall mean the "Consumer Price Index for Urban Wage Earners and Clerical Workers (1967=100)" specified for "All Items. United States" compiled by the Bureau of Labor Statistics of the United States Department of Labor (the "Index"). In the event the Consumer Price Index shall be converted to a different standard reference base or otherwise revised, the determination of the percentage change shall be made with the use of such conversion factor, formula or table for converting the Consumer Price Index as may be published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics or, if said Bureau shall not publish the same, then as shall be reasonably determined by the parties.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a strong in-text contradiction by altering key payment terms. Originally, a fixed fee per gallon was specified with payment due within 30 days of the invoice date, invoiced monthly. The revised text introduces ambiguity by stating the fee is 'to be determined by market conditions,' changes the invoicing period to quarterly, extends the payment timeframe to 60 days, and introduces a condition of 'pending a satisfactory audit.' This directly conflicts with any expectation of a consistent or predictable payment schedule, as alluded to in the original text, especially if relying on a concrete fee, which creates ambiguity and an in-text contradiction regarding payment, the conditions of payment and payment time frame. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
